key_items: Bitcoin crashes to $67K range, down 13% in a week amid ETF outflows and Mt. Gox selling pressure
Strategy (MSTR) reports first BTC sale in years, triggering $400M liquidations and institutional confidence loss
Political headwinds: Sanders, Warren push Labor Department to block crypto 401(k)s, while regulatory uncertainty persists despite SEC's strategic priority announcement
reasoning: Bitcoin faces a confluence of bearish technical and institutional pressures: major liquidations below $68K, significant ETF outflows, Strategy's symbolic BTC sale signaling reduced institutional conviction, and Mt. Gox transfer concerns. While some positive catalysts exist (SEC strategic framework, Charles Schwab's 2027 crypto trading launch, whale accumulation), the near-term technical damage and negative regulatory sentiment from high-profile Democrats outweigh bullish factors.
risks: Continued Mt. Gox liquidations, further institutional capitulation (similar to Strategy's sale), and a macroeconomic shock from weak jobs data on Friday could accelerate Bitcoin toward mid-$60K support levels within the 1-7 day window.
